findings.description: Header form fields were converted from a compact single-row form layout into a vertical stacked list, changing the overall page structure.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, Legal Name, Account Number, Schedule Type, Product Type, Month, and Year appear together across the top in one horizontal form band. In the HTML, these fields are listed one per line beneath the title area, with much larger vertical spacing.

findings.description: Form field styling and emphasis are not preserved in the header section.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses boxed entry fields and tightly aligned labels, with the legal name note italicized inside the form row. The HTML renders these as plain text labels without visible input boxes, reducing visual distinction.

findings.description: The table structure is mostly preserved, but the TOTAL row is not visually aligned the same way as in the PDF.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, TOTAL appears at the bottom right adjacent to the final numeric cells. In the HTML, TOTAL appears shifted left within the table body row rather than clearly aligned with the rightmost total cells.

findings.description: Reading order around the top page elements is slightly altered.
findings.evidence: The PDF presents the form header immediately after the title block, followed by the product type box and then the table. In the HTML, the title is followed by a stacked field list and then a separate Product Type section, making the top content flow differently.

findings.description: The HTML table does not preserve the same number of body rows as the PDF form grid. The source form shows more blank entry lines than the HTML table.
findings.evidence: PDF: the form grid under the column headers contains approximately 19 blank entry rows before the bottom TOTAL row. HTML: the <tbody> contains 16 empty rows plus one final TOTAL row.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is not faithful to the document structure. The main title is split into multiple heading levels and a boxed label is promoted to a section heading.
findings.evidence:
PDF: centered title block reads 'Refiner' then 'Schedule of State Diversion Corrections' with 'North Carolina Department of Revenue' as plain text; the boxed 'Product Type' is a field label/list, not a document section heading. HTML: uses

Refiner

,

Schedule of State Diversion Corrections

, and

Product Type

.

findings.description: The image alt text is only partially accurate and omits visible text from the image/logo area.
findings.evidence: PDF image area includes the North Carolina outline and adjacent text 'Gas-1207DC' and '1-11'. HTML alt text is 'North Carolina map outline with Gas-1207DC and 1-11', which is broadly accurate but not clearly describing the exact logo/placement; acceptable but imperfect.
